title: Datasets
text: MNIST, QMNIST, CIFAR-10, Small NORB, can return list containing matrix numeric data labels separately. Supply = "matrix".

title: Datasets
text: Synthetic data similar used Lee co-workers JSE multi-JSE papers. Martin Wattenberg, Fernanda Viégas Ian Johnson published interactive article use t-SNE Effectively. JavaScript functions used create simulation datasets (can also found https://github.com/distillpub/post--misread-tsne), translated R. RnavGraphImageData package installed, also functions convert Olivetti Frey faces datasets row-based data frame functions visualize . original Isomap Swiss Roll face-pose datasets available via Internet Archive. functions require R.matlab package read Matlab files, face-pose dataset also requires external gzip uncompress command read Unix compress file. Code download visualize MNIST database, based gist Brendan O’Connor, graciously allowed MIT-licensed. QMNIST extends MNIST test dataset 60,000 digits (120,000 digits total). Fashion-MNIST dataset intended drop-replacement MNIST digits database, using images fashion items, harder perform well machine learning benchmarks. Items can visualized function intended MNIST digits. Kuzushiji-MNIST dataset, another drop-replacement MNIST digits, time types cursive Japanese characters. Small NORB dataset, consists pairs images 50 toys different angles different lighting conditions. CIFAR-10 dataset, consists 60,000 32 x 32 color images ten different classes. 3D point cloud mammoth Smithsonian, Understanding UMAP, based work originally done Max Noichl. 3D S-curve hole data set, used validate PaCMAP method (see also github repo). Bonus: translation Python sklearn.datasets.make_s_curvefunction. Also: simple 2D curve dataset, useful testing initialization strategies.

title: Datasets
code: library(snedata)  # Load RnavGraphImageData library(RnavGraphImageData)  # Load the Frey faces dataset with each image as a row frey <- frey_faces() # Display the first pose show_frey_face(frey, 1)  # PCA scores plot, with color indicating the frame index frey_pca <- prcomp(frey[, -561], retx = TRUE, rank. = 2) plot(frey_pca$x, col = frey$color, pch = 16, cex = 0.75)  # Load the Olivetti faces dataset with each image as a row olivetti <- olivetti_faces() # Show the second pose of the first face show_olivetti_face(olivetti, 1, 2)  # fetch the Fashion-MNIST dataset fashion <- download_fashion_mnist()  # Works as a drop-in replacement for the MNIST digits, can repeat the above # view the fifth item etc. show_mnist_digit(fashion, 5)  # similarly for Kuzushiji-MNIST dataset of Japanese cursive characters # (set verbose flag to see download progress) kuzushiji <- download_kuzushiji_mnist(verbose = TRUE) # View the tenth character show_mnist_digit(kuzushiji, 10)  # Download the small NORB dataset norb <- download_norb_small(verbose = TRUE) # View an image, compare with example at https://github.com/ndrplz/small_norb show_norb_object(norb, category = 2, instance = 6, elevation = 6, azimuth = 24, lighting = 2)

what: SNE and Dimensionality Reduction Dataset Functions
title: SNE and Dimensionality Reduction Dataset Functions
text: SNE Dataset Functions R package provides functions generating simple simulation datasets use Stochastic Neighbor Embedding related dimensionality reduction methods, obviously popular t-SNE.

title: SNE and Dimensionality Reduction Dataset Functions
text: package includes simulation datasets, datasets use t-SNE Effectively, optional Frey Olivetti face helpers, MNIST-like datasets, CIFAR-10, Small NORB, mammoth point clouds, 20 Newsgroups, historical Isomap datasets, examples used dimensionality reduction papers. pkgdown site fuller datasets article table, notes, longer examples.

title: SNE and Dimensionality Reduction Dataset Functions
SNE and Dimensionality Reduction Dataset Functions
code: package?snedata # lists all the functions ?snedata::gaussian_data # contains links to all the other distill.pub functions

title: SNE and Dimensionality Reduction Dataset Functions
code: library(snedata)  # 3000 points sampled from the surface of a sphere sphere3000 <- sphere(n = 3000)  # 1500 points sampled from a toroidal helix with 30 coils: helix1500 <- helix(n = 1500, nwinds = 30)  # 1500 points from a filled sphere: ball1500 <- ball(n = 1500)  # 1000 points from a "Swiss Roll" distribution: swiss1000 <- swiss_roll(n = 1000)  # 1000 points from a five-dimensional gaussian: gauss1000 <- gaussian_data(n = 1000, dim = 5)  # Generate datasets similar to those used in the main text of "How to Use t-SNE Effectively" misread_tsne <- list(     two_clusters = two_clusters_data(n = 50, dim = 2),     two_different_sized_clusters = two_different_clusters_data(n = 75, dim = 2),     three_clusters_50 = three_clusters_data(n = 50, dim = 2),     three_clusters_200 = three_clusters_data(n = 200, dim = 2),     gaussian_cloud = gaussian_data(n = 500, dim = 100),     ellipsoidal_gaussian_cloud = long_gaussian_data(n = 100, dim = 50),     two_long_linear_clusters = long_cluster_data(n = 75),     cluster_in_cluster = subset_clusters_data(n = 75, dim = 50),     linked_rings = link_data(n = 100),     trefoil_knot = trefoil_data(n = 150) )  # fetch the MNIST data set from the MNIST website mnist <- download_mnist()  # view the fifth digit show_mnist_digit(mnist, 5)  # first 60,000 instances are the training set mnist_train <- head(mnist, 60000) # the remaining 10,000 are the test set mnist_test <- tail(mnist, 10000)  # PCA on 1000 random training examples mnist_r1000 <- mnist_train[sample(nrow(mnist_train), 1000), ]  pca <- prcomp(mnist_r1000[, 1:784], retx = TRUE, rank. = 2) # plot the scores of the first two components plot(pca$x[, 1:2], type = 'n') text(pca$x[, 1:2], labels = mnist_r1000$Label, cex = 0.5,   col = rainbow(length(levels(mnist_r1000$Label)))[mnist_r1000$Label])  # save to disk save(mnist, file = "mnist.Rda")  # To avoid a very wide data frame for larger image datasets: mnist_matrix <- download_mnist(as = "matrix")  # download the original Isomap Swiss Roll dataset from the Internet Archive # requires the optional R.matlab package isomap_swiss <- download_isomap_swiss_roll()  # download the original Isomap faces dataset from the Internet Archive # requires R.matlab and an external gzip or uncompress command isomap_faces <- download_isomap_faces() show_isomap_face(isomap_faces, 1)

title: Download CIFAR-10 — download_cifar10
text: Download CIFAR-10 database images.

title: Download CIFAR-10 — download_cifar10
code: download_cifar10(   url = "https://www.cs.toronto.edu/~kriz/cifar-10-binary.tar.gz",   destfile = NULL,   cleanup = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE,   as = c("data.frame", "matrix") )

title: Download CIFAR-10 — download_cifar10
text: data frame 3074 variables: r1, r2, r3 ... r1024: Integer pixel value red channel image, 0 255. g1, g2, g3 ... g1024: Integer pixel value green channel image, 0 255. b1, b2, b3 ... b1024: Integer pixel value blue channel image, 0 255. Label: image category, represented factor range 0-9. Description: name image category associated Label, represented factor. pixel features organized row-wise top left image. Label levels correspond following class names (stored Description column): 0: Airplane 1: Automobile 2: Bird 3: Cat 4: Deer 5: Dog 6: Frog 7: Horse 8: Ship 9: Truck 60,000 items data set. first 50,000 training set, remaining 10,000 testing set. Items dataset can visualized show_cifar() function. information see https://www.cs.toronto.edu/~kriz/cifar.html.

title: Download CIFAR-10 — download_cifar10
text: url URL CIFAR-10 data. destfile Filename download CIFAR-10 tarfile. NULL, file temporary work directory used. untarred processed directory. cleanup TRUE, destfile untarred data deleted function returns. worth setting FALSE debug problems. verbose TRUE, download progress logged message. Return format. Use "data.frame" original data frame shape, "matrix" list data, labels, descriptions.

title: Download CIFAR-10 — download_cifar10
text: = "data.frame", data frame containing CIFAR-10 dataset. = "matrix", list data, integer matrix one image per row, labels, factor numeric class labels, descriptions, factor class names.

title: Download CIFAR-10 — download_cifar10
text: Downloads image label files training test datasets converts data frame matrix/list result. CIFAR-10 dataset contains 60000 32 x 32 color images, divided ten different classes, 6000 images per class.

title: Download CIFAR-10 — download_cifar10
text: CIFAR-10 dataset https://www.cs.toronto.edu/~kriz/cifar.html Krizhevsky, ., & Hinton, G. (2009). Learning multiple layers features tiny images (Vol. 1, . 4, p. 7). Technical report, University Toronto.

title: Download CIFAR-10 — download_cifar10
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # download the data set cifar10 <- download_cifar10(verbose = TRUE)  # first 50,000 instances are the training set cifar10_train <- head(cifar10, 50000) # the remaining 10,000 are the test set cifar10_test <- tail(cifar10, 10000)  # PCA on 1000 examples cifar10_r1000 <- cifar10[sample(nrow(cifar10), 1000), ] pca <- prcomp(cifar10_r1000[, 1:(32 * 32)], retx = TRUE, rank. = 2) # plot the scores of the first two components plot(pca$x[, 1:2], type = "n") text(pca$x[, 1:2],   labels = cifar10_r1000$Label,   col = rainbow(length(levels(cifar10$Label)))[cifar10_r1000$Label] ) } # }

title: Download MNIST — download_mnist
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # download the MNIST data set mnist <- download_mnist()  # first 60,000 instances are the training set mnist_train <- head(mnist, 60000) # the remaining 10,000 are the test set mnist_test <- tail(mnist, 10000)  # PCA on 1000 random training examples mnist_r1000 <- mnist_train[sample(nrow(mnist_train), 1000), ] pca <- prcomp(mnist_r1000[, 1:784], retx = TRUE, rank. = 2) # plot the scores of the first two components plot(pca$x[, 1:2], type = "n") text(pca$x[, 1:2],   labels = mnist_r1000$Label,   col = rainbow(length(levels(mnist$Label)))[mnist_r1000$Label] ) } # }

code: download_norb_small(   base_url = "https://cs.nyu.edu/~ylclab/data/norb-v1.0-small/",   verbose = FALSE,   split = c("all", "training", "testing"),   as = c("data.frame", "matrix") )

title: Download Small NORB — download_norb_small
text: data frame 18,439 variables: c0px1, c0px2, c0px3 ... c0px9216: Integer pixel value, 0 (white) 255 (black) first image pair c1px1, c1px2, c1px3 ... c1px9216: Integer pixel value, 0 (white) 255 (black) second image pair Instance: index toy particular category, represented factor range 0-9. training set consists instances 0, 1, 2, 3 5, test set consists 4, 6, 7, 8 9. Elevation: elevation camera represented factor range 0-8. represent elevations 30 70 degrees horizontal, increments 5 degrees. Azimuth: azimuth, represented factor range 0, 2, 4 .. 34. Multiply ten get value degrees. Lighting: lighting condition, represened factor range 0-5. Label: toy category, represented factor range 0-4. Split: Whether toy training testing set, represented factor Description: name toy category associated Label, represented factor. pixel features organized row-wise top left image. Label levels correspond : 0: Four-legged animal 1: Human figure 2: Airplane 3: Truck 4: Car 48,600 items data set. first 24,300 training set, remaining 24,300 testing set, can also use Split column determine split given row . Items dataset can visualized show_norb_object() function. information see https://cs.nyu.edu/~ylclab/data/norb-v1.0-small/.

title: Download Small NORB — download_norb_small
text: base_url Base URL files located . verbose TRUE, download progress logged message. split split download. Use "" training testing sets, "training" "testing" one split. Return format. Use "data.frame" original data frame shape, "matrix" list data meta.

title: Download Small NORB — download_norb_small
text: = "data.frame", data frame containing Small NORB dataset. = "matrix", list data, integer matrix one image pair per row, meta, data frame non-pixel metadata columns.

title: Download Small NORB — download_norb_small
text: Downloads image label files training test datasets converts data frame matrix/list result. Small NORB dataset contains images 50 toys. toys divided five categories (animal, human, airplane, truck, car) ten examples per category. object images 6 different lighting conditions, 9 elevations 18 different azimuths, 972 images per toy. process repeated different camera, actually 1,944 images per toy. dataset stores pair images given toy, lighting, elevation azimuth single row. image 96 96 pixels, first 9,216 columns contain pixels first image, second 9,216 (9217:18432) columns contain pixels second image. information (lighting ) also stored factors.

Learning methods generic object recognition invariance pose lighting. IEEE Computer Society Conference Computer Vision Pattern Recognition (CVPR) 2004 (pp. 97-104). IEEE. http://doi.ieeecomputersociety.org/10.1109/CVPR.2004.144

title: Download Small NORB — download_norb_small
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # download the data set norb <- download_norb_small(verbose = TRUE)  # first 24,300 instances are the training set norb_train <- head(norb, 24300) # the remaining 24,300 are the test set norb_test <- tail(norb, 24300)  # Or equivalently norb_train2 <- norb[norb$Split == "training", ] norb_test2 <- norb[norb$Split == "testing", ]  identical(norb_train, norb_train2) # TRUE identical(norb_test, norb_test2) # also TRUE  # PCA on 1000 examples norb_r1000 <- norb[sample(nrow(norb), 1000), ] pca <- prcomp(norb_r1000[, 1:(96 * 96 * 2)], retx = TRUE, rank. = 2) # plot the scores of the first two components plot(pca$x[, 1:2], type = "n") text(pca$x[, 1:2],   labels = norb_r1000$Label,   col = rainbow(length(levels(norb$Label)))[norb_r1000$Label] ) } # }

title: Download QMNIST — download_qmnist

code: download_qmnist(   base_url = qmnist_url,   verbose = FALSE,   as = c("data.frame", "matrix") )

title: Download QMNIST — download_qmnist
text: data frame 785 variables: px1, px2, px3 ... px784: Integer pixel value, 0 (white) 255 (black). Label: digit represented image, range 0-9. Pixels organized row-wise. Label variable stored factor. 120,000 digits data set. first 60,000 training set, found qmnist-train-images-idx3-ubyte.gz file. remaining 60,000 test set, qmnist-test-images-idx3-ubyte.gz file. Items dataset can visualized show_mnist_digit(). information see https://github.com/facebookresearch/qmnist.

title: Download QMNIST — download_qmnist
text: base_url Base URL QMNIST files located . verbose TRUE, download progress logged message. Return format. Use "data.frame" original data frame shape, "matrix" list data labels.

title: Download QMNIST — download_qmnist
text: = "data.frame", data frame containing QMNIST digits. = "matrix", list data, integer matrix one image per row, labels, factor digit labels.

title: Download QMNIST — download_qmnist
text: Downloads image label files training test datasets https://github.com/facebookresearch/qmnist converts data frame matrix/list result.

title: Download 20 Newsgroups — download_twenty_newsgroups
text: Downloads 20 Newsgroups dataset, contains approximately 20,000 newsgroup documents 20 different newsgroups. distribution approximately balanced.

title: Download 20 Newsgroups — download_twenty_newsgroups
code: download_twenty_newsgroups(   subset = "all",   verbose = FALSE,   tmpdir = NULL,   cleanup = TRUE )

title: Download 20 Newsgroups — download_twenty_newsgroups
text: data frame 6 variables: Id: unique identifier document, consisting subset concatenated position subset, e.g. train_1. FileId: integer identifier document, filename downloaded data. aware unique. Text: full text message including header, footer, quotes. Newlines preserved. Subset: factor two levels: train test, indicating whether document training test subset. Label: newsgroup represented integer id, range 0-19. Newsgroup: factor 20 levels, indicating newsgroup document belongs . labels correspond : 0: alt.atheism 1: comp.graphics 2: comp.os.ms-windows.misc 3: comp.sys.ibm.pc.hardware 4: comp.sys.mac.hardware 5: comp.windows.x 6: misc.forsale 7: rec.autos 8: rec.motorcycles 9: rec.sport.baseball 10: rec.sport.hockey 11: sci.crypt 12: sci.electronics 13: sci.med 14: sci.space 15: soc.religion.christian 16: talk.politics.guns 17: talk.politics.mideast 18: talk.politics.misc 19: talk.religion.misc also present Newsgroup factor. 11,314 items train dataset 7,532 items test total 18,846 items choose subset = "".

title: Download 20 Newsgroups — download_twenty_newsgroups
text: subset string specifying subset dataset download process. Acceptable values "train" training set, "test" test set, "" sets combined. Default "". verbose TRUE, log progress download, extraction processing. tmpdir string specifying directory dataset downloaded extracted. NULL (default), temporary directory used. path provided exist, created. cleanup logical flag indicating whether delete downloaded extracted files processing. TRUE tmpdir created function, tmpdir deleted processing. Default FALSE.

title: Download 20 Newsgroups — download_twenty_newsgroups
text: Data frame containing 20 Newsgroups Data.

title: Download 20 Newsgroups — download_twenty_newsgroups
text: analysis text, want use tools packages tm tidytext. files read latin1 encoding, can still odd control codes messages.

title: Download 20 Newsgroups — download_twenty_newsgroups
text: Lang, K. (1995). Newsweeder: Learning filter netnews. Proceedings Twelfth International Conference Machine Learning 1995 (pp. 331-339). Morgan Kaufmann.

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{  # Download and process the training set ng_train <- download_twenty_newsgroups(subset = "train")  # Download and process both training and test sets, with verbose output ng_all <- download_twenty_newsgroups(subset = "all", verbose = TRUE)  # Download and process the test set, using a specific directory and enabling # cleanup ng_test <- download_twenty_newsgroups(   subset = "test",   tmpdir = "path/to/dir", cleanup = TRUE ) } # }

text: faces datasets originate Sam Roweis' dataset web page: http://www.cs.nyu.edu/~roweis/data.html. Code download visualize MNIST digits fashion datasets originates gist Brendan O'Connor: https://gist.github.com/brendano/39760.

title: snedata: Dataset functions for Stochastic Neighbor Embedding and related dimensionality reduction methods. — snedata
text: simple datasets characterizing behavior various embedding algorithms , Lee et al (2016) Agrafiotis Xu (2002). swiss_roll(). 2D plane curled 3D. sphere(). Points sampled surface 3D sphere. ball(). Points sampled interior 3D sphere. helix(). Points sampled 3D toroidal helix ends jointed together.

what: Simulation functions from "How to use t-SNE Effectively"
title: snedata: Dataset functions for Stochastic Neighbor Embedding and related dimensionality reduction methods. — snedata
text: online article Wattenberg et al (2016), runs t-SNE live browser. Much data pertains investigating behavior clusters gaussians varying dimensionality, heterogeneity relative separation. quite lot functions: circle_data(). 2D circle. cube_data(). cube. gaussian_data(). Gaussian. grid_data(). 2D grid. link_data(). Two linked circles. long_cluster_data(). Two long parallel clusters. long_gaussian_data(). gaussian unequal standard deviation dimensions. ortho_curve(). Points related mutually orthogonal steps. random_circle_cluster_data(). Points sampled circle jittered. random_circle_data(). Points randomly sampled circumference circle. random_jump(). Points generated random walk extra gaussian perturbation. random_walk(). Points generated random walk. simplex_data(). Points arranged rough simplex. subset_clusters_data(). small cluster inside larger one. three_clusters_data(). Points sampled three gaussian clusters unequal relative distances. trefoil_data(). Points sampled shape 3D trefoil knot. two_clusters_data(). Two gaussian clusters. two_different_clusters_data(). Two gaussian clusters, differing standard deviations. unlink_data(). Two unlinked circles.

title: snedata: Dataset functions for Stochastic Neighbor Embedding and related dimensionality reduction methods. — snedata
text: RnavGraphImageData package https://cran.r-project.org/package=RnavGraphImageData installed loaded, functions convert Olivetti Frey faces datasets row-based format, functions visualize images. frey_faces(). Loads Frey faces row-based data frame. show_frey_face(). Display one poses Frey faces. olivetti_faces(). Loads Olivetti faces row-based data frame. show_olivetti_face(). Display one poses Olivetti faces.

title: snedata: Dataset functions for Stochastic Neighbor Embedding and related dimensionality reduction methods. — snedata
text: original Isomap Swiss Roll face-pose datasets can downloaded Internet Archive. download functions require optional packages: R.matlab Matlab files. compressed face data also requires external gzip uncompress command. download_isomap_swiss_roll(). Downloads original Isomap Swiss-roll data row-based data frame. download_isomap_faces(). Downloads original Isomap face-pose data row-based data frame. show_isomap_face(). Display one Isomap faces.

title: snedata: Dataset functions for Stochastic Neighbor Embedding and related dimensionality reduction methods. — snedata
text: Another (much larger) image dataset MNIST digits dataset. original data set described http://yann.lecun.com/exdb/mnist/, package downloads https://github.com/fgnt/mnist mirror. Functions available download dataset data frame matrix/list result visualize individual digits: download_mnist(). Downloads MNIST dataset files row-based data frame default, matrix/list result = "matrix". show_mnist_digit(). Display one MNIST digits.

title: snedata: Dataset functions for Stochastic Neighbor Embedding and related dimensionality reduction methods. — snedata
text: Fashion-MNIST dataset (Xiao et al. 2017) designed drop-replacement

title: snedata: Dataset functions for Stochastic Neighbor Embedding and related dimensionality reduction methods. — snedata
text: Agrafiotis, D. K., & Xu, H. (2002). self-organizing principle learning nonlinear manifolds. Proceedings National Academy Sciences, 99(25), 15869-15872. Lee, J. ., Peluffo-Ordo'nez, D. H., & Verleysen, M. (2015). Multi-scale similarities stochastic neighbour embedding: Reducing dimensionality preserving local global structure. Neurocomputing, 169, 246-261. Wattenberg, M., Vie'gas, F., & Johnson, . (2016) Use t-SNE Effectively. Distill http://doi.org/10.23915/distill.00002 Xiao, H., Kashif, R., & Vollgraf, R. (2017). Fashion-MNIST: Novel Image Dataset Benchmarking Machine Learning Algorithms. arXiv preprint arXiv:1708.07747. https://github.com/zalandoresearch/fashion-mnist/

title: snedata: Dataset functions for Stochastic Neighbor Embedding and related dimensionality reduction methods. — snedata
text: Maintainer: James Melville jlmelville@gmail.com Authors: James Melville jlmelville@gmail.com

title: snedata: Dataset functions for Stochastic Neighbor Embedding and related dimensionality reduction methods. — snedata
code: # 300 points sampled from the surface of a sphere sphere300 <- sphere(n = 300)  # 150 points sampled from a toroidal helix with 30 coils: helix150 <- helix(n = 150, nwinds = 30)  # 150 points from a filled sphere: ball150 <- ball(n = 150)  # 100 points from a "Swiss Roll" distribution: swiss100 <- swiss_roll(n = 100)  # 50 points from a 2D gaussian g2d <- gaussian_data(n = 50, dim = 2)  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Load RnavGraphImageData library(RnavGraphImageData)  # Load the Frey faces dataset with each image as a row frey <- frey_faces() # Display the first pose show_frey_face(frey, 1)  # Load the Olivetti faces dataset with each image as a row olivetti <- olivetti_faces() # Show the second pose of the first face show_olivetti_face(olivetti, 1, 2) } # }  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # fetch the MNIST data set from the configured mirror mnist <- download_mnist() # view the fifth digit show_mnist_digit(mnist, 5)  # first 60,000 instances are the training set mnist_train <- head(mnist, 60000) # the remaining 10,000 are the test set mnist_test <- tail(mnist, 10000)  # PCA on 1000 random training examples mnist_r1000 <- mnist_train[sample(nrow(mnist_train), 1000), ]  pca <- prcomp(mnist_r1000[, 1:784], retx = TRUE, rank. = 2) # plot the scores of the first two components plot(pca$x[, 1:2], type = "n") text(pca$x[, 1:2],   labels = mnist_r1000$Label, cex = 0.5,   col = rainbow(length(levels(mnist_r1000$Label)))[mnist_r1000$Label] )  # save to disk save(mnist, file = "mnist.Rda") } # }  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # fetch the Fashion-MNIST data fashion <- download_fashion_mnist()  # You can repeat the same example as with the MNIST digits example. # Shows the fifth Fashion-MNIST "digit" (actually a dress) show_mnist_digit(fashion, 5)  # and so on... } # }

title: Sphere data set. — sphere
text: dataset like used : Lee, J. ., Peluffo-Ordo'nez, D. H., & Verleysen, M. (2015). Multi-scale similarities stochastic neighbour embedding: Reducing dimensionality preserving local global structure. Neurocomputing, 169, 246-261.

title: Subset Cluster Data — subset_clusters_data
text: One tiny gaussian cluster inside big cluster "Use t-SNE Effectively".

title: Subset Cluster Data — subset_clusters_data
text: n Number points per gaussian. dim Dimension gaussians. big_sdev Standard deviation bigger cluster, default 50. smaller cluster standard deviation 1.

title: Subset Cluster Data — subset_clusters_data
text: Data frame coordinates X1, X2 ... Xdim columns, color color column.

title: Subset Cluster Data — subset_clusters_data
text: Creates dataset consisting two gaussians center, first cluster standard deviation 1, second standard deviation big_sdev (default 50). Points colored depending cluster belong (small cluster dark powder blue, large light orange).

title: Subset Cluster Data — subset_clusters_data
text: http://distill.pub/2016/misread-tsne/

title: Subset Cluster Data — subset_clusters_data
code: df <- subset_clusters_data(n = 50, dim = 2)  # 10D example where the big cluster is only twice the standard deviation of # the small cluster df <- subset_clusters_data(n = 50, dim = 10, big_sdev = 2)

title: Swiss Roll Data Set — swiss_roll
text: Simulation data randomly sampled 2D plane curled 3 dimensions.

title: Swiss Roll Data Set — swiss_roll
code: swiss_roll(n = 1000, min_phi = 1.5 * pi, max_phi = 4.5 * pi, max_z = 10)

title: Swiss Roll Data Set — swiss_roll
text: n Number points create. min_phi Minimum value range phi sample . max_phi Maximum value range phi sample . max_z Maximum value z range sample (minimum values always 0).

title: Swiss Roll Data Set — swiss_roll
text: Data frame x, y, z columns containing coordinates points color RGB color.

title: Swiss Roll Data Set — swiss_roll
text: Creates series points randomly sampled swiss roll-shaped manifold: two-dimensional plane rolled spiral shape. just look swiss roll. formula sampling x, y, z coordinates used dataset given Stochastic Proximity Embedding paper Agrafiotis Xu (know originally came data set, though):   phi sampled 3 * pi / 2 5 * pi / 2, z sampled 0 10. ranges phi z can modified, desired. Points colored based value phi. unrolled manifold flat sheet, see color change linearly direction unrolled . just plot x-y cross section (see examples).
code: x <- phi * cos(phi) y <- phi * sin(phi)

title: Swiss Roll Data Set — swiss_roll
text: first saw equations Agrafiotis, D. K., & Xu, H. (2002). self-organizing principle learning nonlinear manifolds. Proceedings National Academy Sciences, 99(25), 15869-15872. dataset turns everywhere, notably: Roweis, S. T., & Saul, L. K. (2000). Nonlinear dimensionality reduction locally linear embedding. Science, 290(5500), 2323-2326. idea flattening Swiss Roll originate publication, certainly popularized use nonlinear dimensionality reduction. Matlab-formatted version dataset still available : http://web.mit.edu/cocosci/isomap/datasets.html sure exactly parameters used generate , can get something similar calling: swiss_roll(n = 20000, min_phi = 1.5 * pi, max_phi = 4.5 * pi, max_z = 50)

title: Swiss Roll Data Set — swiss_roll
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ swiss1000 <- swiss_roll(n = 1000) # Coloring should be obvious with a 2D plot of the cross section: plot(swiss1000$x, swiss1000$y, col = swiss1000$color, pch = 20) } # }

title: High Dimensional Spheres Dataset — taspheres
text: Creates dataframe consisting samples d-spheres radius r enclosed within larger d-sphere radius 5 * r.

title: High Dimensional Spheres Dataset — taspheres
text: n_samples Number points sample n_spheres d-spheres. larger d-sphere 10 * n_samples points. d dimensionality sphere. returned dataframe d + 1 dimensions Euclidean space sphere embedded. n_spheres Number spheres return. n_spheres - 1 small spheres 1 larger sphere. r radius smaller spheres. larger sphere radius 5 * r.

title: High Dimensional Spheres Dataset — taspheres
text: Data frame d + 1 numerical columns containing coordinates d-spheres "label" factor column giving identity d-sphere: levels 0 .. n_spheres - 2 smaller d-spheres. Level n_spheres - 1 label big d-sphere.

title: High Dimensional Spheres Dataset — taspheres
text: dataset used Moor co-workers "Topological Autoencoders" paper function based Python code github repo paper.

title: High Dimensional Spheres Dataset — taspheres
text: Moor, M., Horn, M., Rieck, B., & Borgwardt, K. (2020). Topological Autoencoders. Proceedings 37th International Conference Machine Learning (ICML) (pp. 7045–7054). PMLR. https://michaelmoor.ml/blog/topoae/main/ https://github.com/BorgwardtLab/topological-autoencoders

title: snedata 0.0.0.9002
text: July 2 2026: Version 0.0.9002 released, featuring: lot tidying , especially around cleaning temp files network connections. instead dataframe, larger datasets, can now return list containing matrix numeric data labels separately. Supply = "matrix". “new” datasets: swiss roll faces dataset Isomap paper, via download_isomap_swiss_roll download_isomap_faces, respectively. Also, function view items faces dataset via show_isomap_face. Probably historical interest , think preserve history. Now vanished MIT webpage, one must rummage Internet Archive, optional R.matlab package installed. faces dataset stored Unix compress file, also need external gzip uncompress command (tried failed get work archive package). Based gist wrote years ago.

title: snedata 0.0.0.9001 and earlier
text: March 2 2025 New link download MNIST digits. original pages Yann LeCun’s website seem gone kaput. Fortunately https://github.com/fgnt/mnist maintained mirror. November 25 2023 Added 20 Newsgroups text dataset. ’ll need look packages like tm tidytext process form suitable embedding. May 14 2022 Added taspheres function generate “spheres” dataset high dimensional spheres nested inside larger sphere, used Topological Autoencoders paper. December 24 2021. Added S-curve hole 2D curve datasets used PaCMAP paper. December 21 2021. Added mammoth datasets Understanding UMAP. June 29 2019. Added QMNIST. March 6 2019. Added CIFAR-10. February 23 2019. Added Small NORB. December 14 2018. Added Kuzushiji-MNIST.
